blocks.statement: I can run a Vernier dynamics-cart trial with a hanging-mass pull, compute predicted v from W_net = ΔKE, measure v with a photogate, and report the % difference with one credible source of error.

blocks.whatWeKnow: We've stated W_net = ΔKE. Today we measure it.
blocks.connection: If our cart confirms the theorem, we can trust it for asteroid energy on Day 19.

blocks.title: Investigation 4.1 — Confirm W_net = ΔKE
blocks.markdown:
**Driving question:** Does the predicted final speed of a cart (from W_net = ΔKE) match the measured speed from the photogate? Across 3 trials with different hanging masses?

**Equipment:** Vernier dynamics track (1 per pair) + 1 cart · pulley clamped at end of track + string · hanging masses (50 g, 100 g, 150 g) · 1 photogate + LabQuest + Logger Pro or Graphical Analysis · balance to weigh cart (record m_cart) and meter stick for d.

**How this lab serves the year's question:** if the theorem holds in our carts, the Day 19 yield calculation is on solid ground.

blocks.title: What you do
steps: Level the track. Weigh the cart and record m_cart.
Set up pulley + string. Hang first mass m_h. Compute F_pull = m_h · g.
Mark the cart's starting position. Measure d to the photogate.
Compute W_net = F_pull · d, predict v from W_net = ½m_cart·v². Compare to measured.
Repeat with the other two hanging masses. Three trials total. Compute % difference.

blocks.title: Safety
blocks.markdown: Hanging masses below knee level. Catch them at end of run. Photogates clamped firmly.

blocks.title: Not in class today?
blocks.markdown: Run the Atwood-machine simulation below — it's the same physics: a hanging mass pulls a load through a distance. Pick a hanging mass, read off the final speed, and compare it to what W_net = ΔKE predicts. Fill the data table from sim values.

blocks.instruction: Sketch your setup: track, cart, pulley, string, hanging mass, photogate. Record m_cart and m_h on the diagram. Fill v_predicted and v_measured as you go.
prompts: Label the cart with m_cart and the hanger with m_h.
Mark where the photogate reads velocity.
Show the pull direction of the string.

blocks.patternPrompt: Three trials with different hanging masses. How close are your predicted and measured speeds?
Did v_predicted match v_measured? Across all 3 trials, within what %?
blocks.interpretPrompt: If there's a gap, what physical thing did NEGATIVE work that we didn't count? Is the gap bigger for smaller or larger hanging masses? And the asteroid connection: how does this serve the year's question?
blocks.frame: My v values matched within ___ %. The most likely source of error was ___, because it would ___ the cart's KE.

blocks.prompt: Write a one-sentence CLAIM about what the lab showed, then one sentence of EVIDENCE (your numbers), then one sentence of REASONING (why the % difference is small).
